title: Avoid full rollout on fernet/credential key rotation
summary: Review #1 feedback from sourcery-ai[bot] addressed: (1) NoHashAnnotations test now asserts specific key absence via NotTo(HaveKey(...)) instead of nil-map check, (2) StablePodTemplate docstring accurately scoped to deterministic-identical-inputs, (3) VolumesMaintained relaxed from strict HaveLen(3) to NotTo(BeEmpty()) + HaveKey/HaveKeyWithValue, (4) E2E fernet and credential rotation tests replaced fixed sleep 10 with 30s polling loop. All 4 CC-0074 unit tests pass. Full controller test suite passes. go vet clean.

tests_checklist.item: Tests are resilient to unrelated changes (no brittle counts)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible
Changes scoped to review feedback only

suggested_improvements: The E2E polling loops (fernet-rotation:124-131, credential-rotation:107-114) compare the Secret API object hash against AFTER_HASH, which was read from the same API object moments before — the loop exits on the first iteration because the API object is already updated by the rotation job. The comment says 'Poll until kubelet projects new keys' but the loop does not actually detect kubelet volume projection (which happens on-disk inside the pod, not on the API object). This is harmless because the downstream assertions (pod UID stability, token validation) do not depend on projection timing, but the comment is slightly misleading. Consider rewording to 'Allow time for kubelet to project new keys' or removing the loop entirely since it provides no actual wait.
